./workshop/history/000116-datatype-aware-navigation-and-creation-via-descriptor.md:13:#115 narrows `<C-g>m` to find datatype artifacts. This issue is the broader follow-up: make parley.nvim a first-class client of ariadne's datatype system, on both the read side (typed pickers) and the write side (template scaffolding for human-driven creation).
./workshop/history/000116-datatype-aware-navigation-and-creation-via-descriptor.md:29:- `<C-g>m` is **kept as the type-blind escape hatch** (unchanged). M2 does only the minimal retrofit: the existing per-type finders (chat/note/issue/vision) source their *home root folder* from the registry instead of hardcoding it. (The faceted typed finder — generic UI + per-type facets — is split to #115.)
./workshop/history/000116-datatype-aware-navigation-and-creation-via-descriptor.md:52:3. **Lua sidecar** at `<prototype>.parley.lua`. Native to nvim, lets the descriptor express dynamic behavior (e.g., a function for slug extraction). Two files per type and it's executable code.
./workshop/history/000116-datatype-aware-navigation-and-creation-via-descriptor.md:128:- **The registry's consumer is a virtual `repo_discovery` skill** (parley.nvim#128).
./workshop/history/000116-datatype-aware-navigation-and-creation-via-descriptor.md:185:- 2026-06-30: closed M3 — M3.1-M3.4 TDD; issues_spec 95/0/0 (parse_issue_new_output + run_sdlc_issue_new), full suite green (make test exit 0), lint 0/0 (237 files). cmd_issue_new (<C-y>c) delegates to `sdlc issue new` (injectable runner; list-form vim.fn.system = no shell injection; robust path parse); dead create_issue removed; render_issue_template retained for the child-decomposition flow (incompatible with sdlc --deps direction + parent-buffer mutation — documented, ariadne#145 unifies). --no-actual: same cross-branch active-time window mis-resolution as M2; cumulative measured at final issue close. **Review-Verdict: FIX-THEN-SHIP → fixed.** The sdlc auto-dispatch ran this time AND an independent fresh-eyes subagent both flagged the same Important (I1): `cmd_issue_new` anchored creation at nvim's cwd, not the git root (regressing #142 — stray dir + colliding ID when run from a subdir). Root-cause fix: forward absolute `--issues-dir`/`--history-dir` (one git-root resolver `resolve_against_git_root`, ARCH-DRY) + a `--` flag-terminator (leading-dash titles) + hardened `parse_issue_new_output` for spaced absolute paths (a consequence of the now-absolute dest). Minors handled: deps/slug forward-API test de-trapped; blocking-push UX noted. issues_spec 98/0/0; full suite green (make test exit 0); lint 0/0.

./workshop/history/000158-bind-tab-in-issue-tracker-to-switch-filter-criteria-close-review.md:1:# Boundary Review — parley.nvim#158 (whole-issue close)
./workshop/history/000158-bind-tab-in-issue-tracker-to-switch-filter-criteria-close-review.md:6:| repo | parley.nvim |
./workshop/history/000158-bind-tab-in-issue-tracker-to-switch-filter-criteria-close-review.md:45:- **Stale tri-state comment survives the scrub** — `lua/parley/init.lua:3002`: `view_mode = 0, -- 0=all (default, done visible), 1=active, 2=all+history`. This is the initializer for the exact field #158 redefines, and it still documents the deleted three-state model. Fix to `-- 0=issues (default), 1=history (#158)`. Behaviorally harmless (default `0` is still correct), but it's the one place the "scrub the tri-state model" cleanup missed — the comment now lies about its own field. (ARCH-PURPOSE: this is the only consumer of the model that wasn't brought in line; everything else derives correctly.)
./workshop/history/000158-bind-tab-in-issue-tracker-to-switch-filter-criteria-close-review.md:53:- **Heads-up, not a #158 defect:** the full `make test` is currently **red** on `tests/unit/tools_builtin_find_spec.lua` ("treats command substitution text in name as data"). That file is untouched, outside the `c0ca6d8..HEAD` window, and **passes in isolation** (`PlenaryBustedFile` → 4/4 Success) — it's a pre-existing full-suite ordering/shared-state flake, not caused by this branch. But the issue's own **Done-when says "Full suite green,"** which is technically unmet right now for that unrelated reason. Worth a `## Log` note so the close evidence isn't overstated.
./workshop/history/000158-bind-tab-in-issue-tracker-to-switch-filter-criteria-close-review.md:60:- **ARCH-PURPOSE: PASS.** Shadow-sweep of the view-mode model: `VIEW_LABELS`, `includes_history`, `filter_for_view`, `M.open`, `config.issue_finder_mappings.cycle_view`, `keybinding_registry`, atlas, and tests all derive from the 2-state model. The sole un-migrated consumer is the `init.lua:3002` comment (documentation, not enforced) — the finding in §4. Purpose fully delivered.
./workshop/history/000158-bind-tab-in-issue-tracker-to-switch-filter-criteria-close-review.md:65:The plan still matches the code — no `## Revisions` entry required. One optional addendum: the plan's checkbox "scrubbed the #152 tri-state comments" is not fully true (`init.lua:3002` remains), so either fix the comment before close or note the residual in `## Log`.

The v1 `system_prompt` fallback that originally lived here was removed in the boundary-review round — see Revisions.)_

./workshop/history/000165-visual-selection-definition-search-should-anchor-at-right-column-close-review.md:711:- **Vim ex-commands that take an implicit current-buffer arg (`:undojoin`, `:write`, `:edit`, etc.) silently target the wrong buffer when called from async/scheduled callbacks.** `helpers.undojoin(buf)` accepted a buf param but called `vim.cmd.undojoin` directly — `:undojoin` operates on the current buffer, ignoring the param. The streaming path looked like it worked because users stay focused on the chat buffer during streaming; the longer-cadence spinner timer was more likely to fire during transient focus changes (autocmds, window switches), and its joins silently went to the wrong buffer. Fix: wrap in `vim.api.nvim_buf_call(buf, function() vim.cmd.undojoin() end)`. Rule: any helper that takes a `buf` parameter and dispatches a Vim ex-command must use `nvim_buf_call` — passing the param to the helper without enforcing buffer context is a contract the helper isn't actually upholding. Spotted in #80 second-pass debugging.
./workshop/history/000165-visual-selection-definition-search-should-anchor-at-right-column-close-review.md:712:- **Sanitized snapshot in `M.get_agent` (init.lua:3570) is an allow-list, not a passthrough.** Every new field added to the agent config schema must also be appended to this snapshot, or it is silently dropped before `agent_info.resolve` ever sees it. This bit #81 (tools/max_tool_iterations/tool_result_max_bytes) and bit #118 again (synthetic_system_prompt/synthetic_system_prompt_ack) — same vector. Rule: when adding a new agent-config field, grep for `M.get_agent = function` and add it there too; ship a regression test that walks `agent record → get_agent → get_agent_info → final usage` (see `tests/unit/config_tools_spec.lua` "get_agent forwards synthetic_system_prompt config" for the pattern).
./workshop/history/000165-visual-selection-definition-search-should-anchor-at-right-column-close-review.md:719:- **In autocmd callbacks, use `nvim_buf_get_name(buf)` not `ev.file`.** `ev.file` can be a relative path when the user opened the file with a relative path (e.g. `nvim workshop/file.md`). `nvim_buf_get_name(buf)` always returns the absolute path. This caused `not_chat()` to fail silently because `find_chat_root` couldn't match the relative path against configured roots.
./workshop/history/000165-visual-selection-definition-search-should-anchor-at-right-column-close-review.md:720:- **After `nvim_buf_set_name` + rename, do `write!` then `edit!`.** `nvim_buf_set_name` marks the buffer as a "new file" at the new path. Without `edit!` to reload, the next manual `:w` warns "file already exists". The `write!` forces the initial write, and `edit!` clears the new-file flag.
./workshop/history/000165-visual-selection-definition-search-should-anchor-at-right-column-close-review.md:748:- Fast-event callbacks: no direct `nvim_*` APIs — use `vim.schedule`
./workshop/history/000165-visual-selection-definition-search-should-anchor-at-right-column-close-review.md:774:- After `edit`, use `nvim_get_current_buf()` not stale buffer variable
./workshop/history/000165-visual-selection-definition-search-should-anchor-at-right-column-close-review.md:811:- **Run the FULL `make test` (lint + unit + integration) before claiming "suite green" in `--verified` — running specs individually skips the lint gate.** This session I ran each `PlenaryBustedFile` spec directly (all green) and wrote "go test/full suite green" in the close evidence — but never ran `make test`, whose FIRST target is `lint` (luacheck). A new `while pos <= #line do` (every branch returns → luacheck 542 "loop executed at most once") failed luacheck, so `make test` was RED at the gate while my Log claimed green. The boundary review caught it (FIX-THEN-SHIP), but nothing *prevented* the premature claim. Rule: the evidence for "green" is a full `make test` exit 0, not a hand-picked set of specs; individual `PlenaryBustedFile` runs are for the red→green TDD loop, not the final gate. (Bonus: luacheck flags a `while` whose body always returns — use `if` and let the iterator's repeated closure-calls do the looping.)
./workshop/history/000165-visual-selection-definition-search-should-anchor-at-right-column-close-review.md:816:- **Raw `nvim_buf_set_text` is arch-forbidden (empty allow-list in `tests/arch/buffer_mutation_spec.lua`); `nvim_buf_set_lines` is allowed in `init.lua`.** #90's buffer-mutation boundary confines span edits to `buffer_edit.lua` (set_text allow-list is `{}` = zero uses). My first R1 cut used `nvim_buf_set_text` for the `[term]` wrap → `make test` red on the arch spec (lint was clean, so I only caught it at the full-suite run, not the unit loop). Fix: wrap via `nvim_buf_set_lines` (rewrite the affected whole line(s)) — the same primitive `drill_in_visual` already uses to wrap a selection, and it's on the set_lines allow-list for `init.lua`. Rule: before reaching for `nvim_buf_set_text`/`set_lines` in a non-`buffer_edit` file, check `tests/arch/buffer_mutation_spec.lua` — prefer the set_lines whole-line rewrite that existing wrappers use.
./workshop/history/000165-visual-selection-definition-search-should-anchor-at-right-column-close-review.md:817:- **To make a decoration-only action undoable, anchor it to a text edit + reuse `projection` — `u` reverts text, never extmarks/diagnostics.** The operator wanted the define highlight+diagnostic undoable; native `u` can't touch pure decorations (review's are undoable only because a round edits text). Resolution: a minimal `[term]` bracket (one `set_lines` = one undo entry) as the anchor + reuse `skills/review/projection.lua` (`record_empty_for(pre)` + `record(post)` + `ensure_watch`). Caveat that shaped the design: `skill_render.snapshot`/`apply_snapshot` are **line-granular**, so the highlight must be whole-line `DiffChange` to round-trip; a column-precise span would not survive undo/redo. In headless tests, `:undo` does NOT fire `TextChanged` — `nvim_exec_autocmds("TextChanged", {buffer=buf})` to drive the projection watcher deterministically.

./workshop/history/000118-synthetic-system-prompt-via-leading-user-turn.md:132:- **Bug found in manual verification**: enabling the flag on an agent had no observable effect. Root cause: `M.get_agent` in `lua/parley/init.lua:3570` returns a *sanitized snapshot* of the agent record — explicitly forwarding `tools` / `max_tool_iterations` / `tool_result_max_bytes` and dropping anything else. New fields silently fell off here before `agent_info.resolve` could see them. Same vector as the M1 tools-forwarding bug already documented in the comment above that block. Fix: added the two synthetic fields to the snapshot allow-list. Regression test added in `tests/unit/config_tools_spec.lua` (`describe "get_agent forwards synthetic_system_prompt config"`) so the next field added to agent_info is forced to walk this path or be caught.
./workshop/history/000130-create-visualization-of-change-log-in-a-pair-session.md:16:2/ and it would open an nvim window, full screen to display such change log. 
./workshop/history/000130-create-visualization-of-change-log-in-a-pair-session.md:17:3/ this nvim is readonly, like the scrollback. 
./workshop/history/000160-navigate-ariadne-artifact-references-close-review.md:1:# Boundary Review — parley.nvim#160 (whole-issue close)
./workshop/history/000160-navigate-ariadne-artifact-references-close-review.md:6:| repo | parley.nvim |
./workshop/history/000160-navigate-ariadne-artifact-references-close-review.md:39:- **Keymap binding only asserted via help text.** `keybindings_spec.lua:46` checks the registry-derived *help line* for `<C-g>r`, which passes even if the `resolve_ref` callback wiring (`init.lua:1973`, `:2221`) were absent. Plan Task 2.3 Step 3 suggested a `maparg` assertion; wiring is present so risk is low.
./workshop/history/000160-navigate-ariadne-artifact-references-close-review.md:62:| repo | parley.nvim |
./workshop/history/000160-navigate-ariadne-artifact-references-close-review.md:106:- **Keymap wiring asserted only via help text.** `keybindings_spec.lua:46-49` checks the registry-derived help lines for `<C-g>r`/`gf`; the actual `resolve_ref`/`resolve_ref_gf` callback wiring (`init.lua:1973`, `:1974`, `:2222`, `:2223`) isn't `maparg`-asserted. Wiring is present in source, so risk is low.

./workshop/history/000163-repo-mode-status-bar-improvements-close-review.md:371:/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,220p' /Users/xianxu/workspace/ariadne/construct/adapted/superpowers-using-superpowers/SKILL.md" in /Users/xianxu/workspace/parley.nvim
./workshop/history/000163-repo-mode-status-bar-improvements-close-review.md:492:/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,200p' workshop/lessons.md" in /Users/xianxu/workspace/parley.nvim
./workshop/history/000163-repo-mode-status-bar-improvements-close-review.md:516:- **Vim ex-commands that take an implicit current-buffer arg (`:undojoin`, `:write`, `:edit`, etc.) silently target the wrong buffer when called from async/scheduled callbacks.** `helpers.undojoin(buf)` accepted a buf param but called `vim.cmd.undojoin` directly — `:undojoin` operates on the current buffer, ignoring the param. The streaming path looked like it worked because users stay focused on the chat buffer during streaming; the longer-cadence spinner timer was more likely to fire during transient focus changes (autocmds, window switches), and its joins silently went to the wrong buffer. Fix: wrap in `vim.api.nvim_buf_call(buf, function() vim.cmd.undojoin() end)`. Rule: any helper that takes a `buf` parameter and dispatches a Vim ex-command must use `nvim_buf_call` — passing the param to the helper without enforcing buffer context is a contract the helper isn't actually upholding. Spotted in #80 second-pass debugging.
./workshop/history/000163-repo-mode-status-bar-improvements-close-review.md:517:- **Sanitized snapshot in `M.get_agent` (init.lua:3570) is an allow-list, not a passthrough.** Every new field added to the agent config schema must also be appended to this snapshot, or it is silently dropped before `agent_info.resolve` ever sees it. This bit #81 (tools/max_tool_iterations/tool_result_max_bytes) and bit #118 again (synthetic_system_prompt/synthetic_system_prompt_ack) — same vector. Rule: when adding a new agent-config field, grep for `M.get_agent = function` and add it there too; ship a regression test that walks `agent record → get_agent → get_agent_info → final usage` (see `tests/unit/config_tools_spec.lua` "get_agent forwards synthetic_system_prompt config" for the pattern).
./workshop/history/000163-repo-mode-status-bar-improvements-close-review.md:524:- **In autocmd callbacks, use `nvim_buf_get_name(buf)` not `ev.file`.** `ev.file` can be a relative path when the user opened the file with a relative path (e.g. `nvim workshop/file.md`). `nvim_buf_get_name(buf)` always returns the absolute path. This caused `not_chat()` to fail silently because `find_chat_root` couldn't match the relative path against configured roots.
./workshop/history/000163-repo-mode-status-bar-improvements-close-review.md:525:- **After `nvim_buf_set_name` + rename, do `write!` then `edit!`.** `nvim_buf_set_name` marks the buffer as a "new file" at the new path. Without `edit!` to reload, the next manual `:w` warns "file already exists". The `write!` forces the initial write, and `edit!` clears the new-file flag.
./workshop/history/000163-repo-mode-status-bar-improvements-close-review.md:553:- Fast-event callbacks: no direct `nvim_*` APIs — use `vim.schedule`
./workshop/history/000163-repo-mode-status-bar-improvements-close-review.md:579:- After `edit`, use `nvim_get_current_buf()` not stale buffer variable
./workshop/history/000163-repo-mode-status-bar-improvements-close-review.md:616:- **Run the FULL `make test` (lint + unit + integration) before claiming "suite green" in `--verified` — running specs individually skips the lint gate.** This session I ran each `PlenaryBustedFile` spec directly (all green) and wrote "go test/full suite green" in the close evidence — but never ran `make test`, whose FIRST target is `lint` (luacheck). A new `while pos <= #line do` (every branch returns → luacheck 542 "loop executed at most once") failed luacheck, so `make test` was RED at the gate while my Log claimed green. The boundary review caught it (FIX-THEN-SHIP), but nothing *prevented* the premature claim. Rule: the evidence for "green" is a full `make test` exit 0, not a hand-picked set of specs; individual `PlenaryBustedFile` runs are for the red→green TDD loop, not the final gate. (Bonus: luacheck flags a `while` whose body always returns — use `if` and let the iterator's repeated closure-calls do the looping.)
./workshop/history/000163-repo-mode-status-bar-improvements-close-review.md:621:- **Raw `nvim_buf_set_text` is arch-forbidden (empty allow-list in `tests/arch/buffer_mutation_spec.lua`); `nvim_buf_set_lines` is allowed in `init.lua`.** #90's buffer-mutation boundary confines span edits to `buffer_edit.lua` (set_text allow-list is `{}` = zero uses). My first R1 cut used `nvim_buf_set_text` for the `[term]` wrap → `make test` red on the arch spec (lint was clean, so I only caught it at the full-suite run, not the unit loop). Fix: wrap via `nvim_buf_set_lines` (rewrite the affected whole line(s)) — the same primitive `drill_in_visual` already uses to wrap a selection, and it's on the set_lines allow-list for `init.lua`. Rule: before reaching for `nvim_buf_set_text`/`set_lines` in a non-`buffer_edit` file, check `tests/arch/buffer_mutation_spec.lua` — prefer the set_lines whole-line rewrite that existing wrappers use.
./workshop/history/000163-repo-mode-status-bar-improvements-close-review.md:622:- **To make a decoration-only action undoable, anchor it to a text edit + reuse `projection` — `u` reverts text, never extmarks/diagnostics.** The operator wanted the define highlight+diagnostic undoable; native `u` can't touch pure decorations (review's are undoable only because a round edits text). Resolution: a minimal `[term]` bracket (one `set_lines` = one undo entry) as the anchor + reuse `skills/review/projection.lua` (`record_empty_for(pre)` + `record(post)` + `ensure_watch`). Caveat that shaped the design: `skill_render.snapshot`/`apply_snapshot` are **line-granular**, so the highlight must be whole-line `DiffChange` to round-trip; a column-precise span would not survive undo/redo. In headless tests, `:undo` does NOT fire `TextChanged` — `nvim_exec_autocmds("TextChanged", {buffer=buf})` to drive the projection watcher deterministically.

./workshop/history/000080-make-the-undo-history-simpler.md:35:- [x] Fix `helpers.undojoin` buffer-context bug: wrap `vim.cmd.undojoin` in `nvim_buf_call(buf, ...)` so the marker reliably lands on the target buffer regardless of which buffer is current. (Discovered in second-pass debugging — see Log.)
./workshop/history/000080-make-the-undo-history-simpler.md:55:  1. `helpers.undojoin(buf)` was calling `vim.cmd.undojoin` directly. `:undojoin` is a Vim command that operates on the *current* buffer; the `buf` parameter was only used for the loaded-check guard. When the spinner timer fired while focus had transiently moved (autocmds firing in another buffer, scheduled callbacks crossing windows), the join marker landed on the wrong buffer and the chat-buffer write created a fresh undo entry. Fix: wrap in `nvim_buf_call(buf, function() vim.cmd.undojoin() end)`. This benefits the streaming path too — that code probably worked-by-luck because users tend to stay focused on the chat buffer during streaming.

./workshop/history/000166-visual-selection-definition-system-manages-footnote-close-review.md:1360:/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,220p' workshop/lessons.md" in /Users/xianxu/workspace/parley.nvim
./workshop/history/000166-visual-selection-definition-system-manages-footnote-close-review.md:1384:- **Vim ex-commands that take an implicit current-buffer arg (`:undojoin`, `:write`, `:edit`, etc.) silently target the wrong buffer when called from async/scheduled callbacks.** `helpers.undojoin(buf)` accepted a buf param but called `vim.cmd.undojoin` directly — `:undojoin` operates on the current buffer, ignoring the param. The streaming path looked like it worked because users stay focused on the chat buffer during streaming; the longer-cadence spinner timer was more likely to fire during transient focus changes (autocmds, window switches), and its joins silently went to the wrong buffer. Fix: wrap in `vim.api.nvim_buf_call(buf, function() vim.cmd.undojoin() end)`. Rule: any helper that takes a `buf` parameter and dispatches a Vim ex-command must use `nvim_buf_call` — passing the param to the helper without enforcing buffer context is a contract the helper isn't actually upholding. Spotted in #80 second-pass debugging.
./workshop/history/000166-visual-selection-definition-system-manages-footnote-close-review.md:1385:- **Sanitized snapshot in `M.get_agent` (init.lua:3570) is an allow-list, not a passthrough.** Every new field added to the agent config schema must also be appended to this snapshot, or it is silently dropped before `agent_info.resolve` ever sees it. This bit #81 (tools/max_tool_iterations/tool_result_max_bytes) and bit #118 again (synthetic_system_prompt/synthetic_system_prompt_ack) — same vector. Rule: when adding a new agent-config field, grep for `M.get_agent = function` and add it there too; ship a regression test that walks `agent record → get_agent → get_agent_info → final usage` (see `tests/unit/config_tools_spec.lua` "get_agent forwards synthetic_system_prompt config" for the pattern).
./workshop/history/000166-visual-selection-definition-system-manages-footnote-close-review.md:1392:- **In autocmd callbacks, use `nvim_buf_get_name(buf)` not `ev.file`.** `ev.file` can be a relative path when the user opened the file with a relative path (e.g. `nvim workshop/file.md`). `nvim_buf_get_name(buf)` always returns the absolute path. This caused `not_chat()` to fail silently because `find_chat_root` couldn't match the relative path against configured roots.
./workshop/history/000166-visual-selection-definition-system-manages-footnote-close-review.md:1393:- **After `nvim_buf_set_name` + rename, do `write!` then `edit!`.** `nvim_buf_set_name` marks the buffer as a "new file" at the new path. Without `edit!` to reload, the next manual `:w` warns "file already exists". The `write!` forces the initial write, and `edit!` clears the new-file flag.
./workshop/history/000166-visual-selection-definition-system-manages-footnote-close-review.md:1421:- Fast-event callbacks: no direct `nvim_*` APIs — use `vim.schedule`
./workshop/history/000166-visual-selection-definition-system-manages-footnote-close-review.md:1447:- After `edit`, use `nvim_get_current_buf()` not stale buffer variable
./workshop/history/000166-visual-selection-definition-system-manages-footnote-close-review.md:1484:- **Run the FULL `make test` (lint + unit + integration) before claiming "suite green" in `--verified` — running specs individually skips the lint gate.** This session I ran each `PlenaryBustedFile` spec directly (all green) and wrote "go test/full suite green" in the close evidence — but never ran `make test`, whose FIRST target is `lint` (luacheck). A new `while pos <= #line do` (every branch returns → luacheck 542 "loop executed at most once") failed luacheck, so `make test` was RED at the gate while my Log claimed green. The boundary review caught it (FIX-THEN-SHIP), but nothing *prevented* the premature claim. Rule: the evidence for "green" is a full `make test` exit 0, not a hand-picked set of specs; individual `PlenaryBustedFile` runs are for the red→green TDD loop, not the final gate. (Bonus: luacheck flags a `while` whose body always returns — use `if` and let the iterator's repeated closure-calls do the looping.)
./workshop/history/000166-visual-selection-definition-system-manages-footnote-close-review.md:1489:- **Raw `nvim_buf_set_text` is arch-forbidden (empty allow-list in `tests/arch/buffer_mutation_spec.lua`); `nvim_buf_set_lines` is allowed in `init.lua`.** #90's buffer-mutation boundary confines span edits to `buffer_edit.lua` (set_text allow-list is `{}` = zero uses). My first R1 cut used `nvim_buf_set_text` for the `[term]` wrap → `make test` red on the arch spec (lint was clean, so I only caught it at the full-suite run, not the unit loop). Fix: wrap via `nvim_buf_set_lines` (rewrite the affected whole line(s)) — the same primitive `drill_in_visual` already uses to wrap a selection, and it's on the set_lines allow-list for `init.lua`. Rule: before reaching for `nvim_buf_set_text`/`set_lines` in a non-`buffer_edit` file, check `tests/arch/buffer_mutation_spec.lua` — prefer the set_lines whole-line rewrite that existing wrappers use.
./workshop/history/000166-visual-selection-definition-system-manages-footnote-close-review.md:1490:- **To make a decoration-only action undoable, anchor it to a text edit + reuse `projection` — `u` reverts text, never extmarks/diagnostics.** The operator wanted the define highlight+diagnostic undoable; native `u` can't touch pure decorations (review's are undoable only because a round edits text). Resolution: a minimal `[term]` bracket (one `set_lines` = one undo entry) as the anchor + reuse `skills/review/projection.lua` (`record_empty_for(pre)` + `record(post)` + `ensure_watch`). Caveat that shaped the design: `skill_render.snapshot`/`apply_snapshot` are **line-granular**, so the highlight must be whole-line `DiffChange` to round-trip; a column-precise span would not survive undo/redo. In headless tests, `:undo` does NOT fire `TextChanged` — `nvim_exec_autocmds("TextChanged", {buffer=buf})` to drive the projection watcher deterministically.

./workshop/history/000166-visual-selection-definition-system-manages-footnote-close-review.md:3975:- [lua/parley/define.lua](/Users/xianxu/workspace/parley.nvim/lua/parley/define.lua:221) keeps footer stripping pure and shares it across message construction, satisfying ARCH-DRY.
./workshop/history/000166-visual-selection-definition-system-manages-footnote-close-review.md:3976:- [lua/parley/init.lua](/Users/xianxu/workspace/parley.nvim/lua/parley/init.lua:1640) leaves Neovim IO in `render_definition` while the document transform lives in `define.lua`, satisfying ARCH-PURE.
./workshop/history/000166-visual-selection-definition-system-manages-footnote-close-review.md:3977:- [tests/unit/build_messages_spec.lua](/Users/xianxu/workspace/parley.nvim/tests/unit/build_messages_spec.lua:707) covers user/assistant stripping and ordinary `---` preservation.
./workshop/history/000166-visual-selection-definition-system-manages-footnote-close-review.md:3978:- [tests/unit/build_messages_spec.lua](/Users/xianxu/workspace/parley.nvim/tests/unit/build_messages_spec.lua:1605) adds live `build_messages_from_model` coverage, avoiding the #155 seam gap.
./workshop/history/000166-visual-selection-definition-system-manages-footnote-close-review.md:3981:- [lua/parley/define.lua](/Users/xianxu/workspace/parley.nvim/lua/parley/define.lua:276): `apply_definition_footnote` always inserts `[^id]` after the selected span. If the line is already `ASIN[^asin] is here` and the user re-defines `ASIN`, it produces `ASIN[^asin][^asin] is here`. That violates the spec’s redefinition behavior in [workshop/issues/000166-visual-selection-definition-system-manages-footnote.md](/Users/xianxu/workspace/parley.nvim/workshop/issues/000166-visual-selection-definition-system-manages-footnote.md:44) and under-delivers ARCH-PURPOSE. Fix sketch: detect an existing immediate `ref` after the selected span and skip reinserting it while still updating the footer; adjust `diagnostic_span.end_col` accordingly; add pure and integration regression tests for this exact flow.
./workshop/history/000166-visual-selection-definition-system-manages-footnote-close-review.md:3984:- [README.md](/Users/xianxu/workspace/parley.nvim/README.md:118): README still says visual `<M-CR>` inline definitions write “nothing” to the transcript. This issue changes user-facing behavior to durable `term[^id]` plus managed footer, so the README docs gate is missing an update for that surface.
./workshop/history/000166-visual-selection-definition-system-manages-footnote-close-review.md:3987:- [workshop/plans/000166-visual-selection-definition-system-manages-footnote-plan.md](/Users/xianxu/workspace/parley.nvim/workshop/plans/000166-visual-selection-definition-system-manages-footnote-plan.md:17): Core concept tables omit an explicit `Kind` column, though the headings separate pure and integration entities.
./workshop/history/000166-visual-selection-definition-system-manages-footnote-close-review.md:3990:- I ran `nvim --headless -c "PlenaryBustedFile tests/unit/define_spec.lua"`: 21 passed, 0 failed.
./workshop/history/000166-visual-selection-definition-system-manages-footnote-close-review.md:4012:- [lua/parley/define.lua](/Users/xianxu/workspace/parley.nvim/lua/parley/define.lua:221) keeps footer stripping pure and shares it across message construction, satisfying ARCH-DRY.
./workshop/history/000166-visual-selection-definition-system-manages-footnote-close-review.md:4013:- [lua/parley/init.lua](/Users/xianxu/workspace/parley.nvim/lua/parley/init.lua:1640) leaves Neovim IO in `render_definition` while the document transform lives in `define.lua`, satisfying ARCH-PURE.
./workshop/history/000166-visual-selection-definition-system-manages-footnote-close-review.md:4014:- [tests/unit/build_messages_spec.lua](/Users/xianxu/workspace/parley.nvim/tests/unit/build_messages_spec.lua:707) covers user/assistant stripping and ordinary `---` preservation.
./workshop/history/000166-visual-selection-definition-system-manages-footnote-close-review.md:4015:- [tests/unit/build_messages_spec.lua](/Users/xianxu/workspace/parley.nvim/tests/unit/build_messages_spec.lua:1605) adds live `build_messages_from_model` coverage, avoiding the #155 seam gap.
./workshop/history/000166-visual-selection-definition-system-manages-footnote-close-review.md:4018:- [lua/parley/define.lua](/Users/xianxu/workspace/parley.nvim/lua/parley/define.lua:276): `apply_definition_footnote` always inserts `[^id]` after the selected span. If the line is already `ASIN[^asin] is here` and the user re-defines `ASIN`, it produces `ASIN[^asin][^asin] is here`. That violates the spec’s redefinition behavior in [workshop/issues/000166-visual-selection-definition-system-manages-footnote.md](/Users/xianxu/workspace/parley.nvim/workshop/issues/000166-visual-selection-definition-system-manages-footnote.md:44) and under-delivers ARCH-PURPOSE. Fix sketch: detect an existing immediate `ref` after the selected span and skip reinserting it while still updating the footer; adjust `diagnostic_span.end_col` accordingly; add pure and integration regression tests for this exact flow.
./workshop/history/000166-visual-selection-definition-system-manages-footnote-close-review.md:4021:- [README.md](/Users/xianxu/workspace/parley.nvim/README.md:118): README still says visual `<M-CR>` inline definitions write “nothing” to the transcript. This issue changes user-facing behavior to durable `term[^id]` plus managed footer, so the README docs gate is missing an update for that surface.

This keeps call sites semantically clear and avoids adding new direct `nvim_buf_set_lines` callers.

./workshop/history/000166-visual-selection-definition-system-manages-footnote-close-review.md:6604:+/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,220p' workshop/lessons.md" in /Users/xianxu/workspace/parley.nvim
./workshop/history/000166-visual-selection-definition-system-manages-footnote-close-review.md:6628:+- **Vim ex-commands that take an implicit current-buffer arg (`:undojoin`, `:write`, `:edit`, etc.) silently target the wrong buffer when called from async/scheduled callbacks.** `helpers.undojoin(buf)` accepted a buf param but called `vim.cmd.undojoin` directly — `:undojoin` operates on the current buffer, ignoring the param. The streaming path looked like it worked because users stay focused on the chat buffer during streaming; the longer-cadence spinner timer was more likely to fire during transient focus changes (autocmds, window switches), and its joins silently went to the wrong buffer. Fix: wrap in `vim.api.nvim_buf_call(buf, function() vim.cmd.undojoin() end)`. Rule: any helper that takes a `buf` parameter and dispatches a Vim ex-command must use `nvim_buf_call` — passing the param to the helper without enforcing buffer context is a contract the helper isn't actually upholding. Spotted in #80 second-pass debugging.
./workshop/history/000166-visual-selection-definition-system-manages-footnote-close-review.md:6629:+- **Sanitized snapshot in `M.get_agent` (init.lua:3570) is an allow-list, not a passthrough.** Every new field added to the agent config schema must also be appended to this snapshot, or it is silently dropped before `agent_info.resolve` ever sees it. This bit #81 (tools/max_tool_iterations/tool_result_max_bytes) and bit #118 again (synthetic_system_prompt/synthetic_system_prompt_ack) — same vector. Rule: when adding a new agent-config field, grep for `M.get_agent = function` and add it there too; ship a regression test that walks `agent record → get_agent → get_agent_info → final usage` (see `tests/unit/config_tools_spec.lua` "get_agent forwards synthetic_system_prompt config" for the pattern).
./workshop/history/000166-visual-selection-definition-system-manages-footnote-close-review.md:6636:+- **In autocmd callbacks, use `nvim_buf_get_name(buf)` not `ev.file`.** `ev.file` can be a relative path when the user opened the file with a relative path (e.g. `nvim workshop/file.md`). `nvim_buf_get_name(buf)` always returns the absolute path. This caused `not_chat()` to fail silently because `find_chat_root` couldn't match the relative path against configured roots.
./workshop/history/000166-visual-selection-definition-system-manages-footnote-close-review.md:6637:+- **After `nvim_buf_set_name` + rename, do `write!` then `edit!`.** `nvim_buf_set_name` marks the buffer as a "new file" at the new path. Without `edit!` to reload, the next manual `:w` warns "file already exists". The `write!` forces the initial write, and `edit!` clears the new-file flag.
./workshop/history/000166-visual-selection-definition-system-manages-footnote-close-review.md:6665:+- Fast-event callbacks: no direct `nvim_*` APIs — use `vim.schedule`
./workshop/history/000166-visual-selection-definition-system-manages-footnote-close-review.md:6691:+- After `edit`, use `nvim_get_current_buf()` not stale buffer variable
./workshop/history/000166-visual-selection-definition-system-manages-footnote-close-review.md:6728:+- **Run the FULL `make test` (lint + unit + integration) before claiming "suite green" in `--verified` — running specs individually skips the lint gate.** This session I ran each `PlenaryBustedFile` spec directly (all green) and wrote "go test/full suite green" in the close evidence — but never ran `make test`, whose FIRST target is `lint` (luacheck). A new `while pos <= #line do` (every branch returns → luacheck 542 "loop executed at most once") failed luacheck, so `make test` was RED at the gate while my Log claimed green. The boundary review caught it (FIX-THEN-SHIP), but nothing *prevented* the premature claim. Rule: the evidence for "green" is a full `make test` exit 0, not a hand-picked set of specs; individual `PlenaryBustedFile` runs are for the red→green TDD loop, not the final gate. (Bonus: luacheck flags a `while` whose body always returns — use `if` and let the iterator's repeated closure-calls do the looping.)
./workshop/history/000166-visual-selection-definition-system-manages-footnote-close-review.md:6733:+- **Raw `nvim_buf_set_text` is arch-forbidden (empty allow-list in `tests/arch/buffer_mutation_spec.lua`); `nvim_buf_set_lines` is allowed in `init.lua`.** #90's buffer-mutation boundary confines span edits to `buffer_edit.lua` (set_text allow-list is `{}` = zero uses). My first R1 cut used `nvim_buf_set_text` for the `[term]` wrap → `make test` red on the arch spec (lint was clean, so I only caught it at the full-suite run, not the unit loop). Fix: wrap via `nvim_buf_set_lines` (rewrite the affected whole line(s)) — the same primitive `drill_in_visual` already uses to wrap a selection, and it's on the set_lines allow-list for `init.lua`. Rule: before reaching for `nvim_buf_set_text`/`set_lines` in a non-`buffer_edit` file, check `tests/arch/buffer_mutation_spec.lua` — prefer the set_lines whole-line rewrite that existing wrappers use.
./workshop/history/000166-visual-selection-definition-system-manages-footnote-close-review.md:6734:+- **To make a decoration-only action undoable, anchor it to a text edit + reuse `projection` — `u` reverts text, never extmarks/diagnostics.** The operator wanted the define highlight+diagnostic undoable; native `u` can't touch pure decorations (review's are undoable only because a round edits text). Resolution: a minimal `[term]` bracket (one `set_lines` = one undo entry) as the anchor + reuse `skills/review/projection.lua` (`record_empty_for(pre)` + `record(post)` + `ensure_watch`). Caveat that shaped the design: `skill_render.snapshot`/`apply_snapshot` are **line-granular**, so the highlight must be whole-line `DiffChange` to round-trip; a column-precise span would not survive undo/redo. In headless tests, `:undo` does NOT fire `TextChanged` — `nvim_exec_autocmds("TextChanged", {buffer=buf})` to drive the projection watcher deterministically.

./workshop/history/000166-visual-selection-definition-system-manages-footnote-close-review.md:9219:+- [lua/parley/define.lua](/Users/xianxu/workspace/parley.nvim/lua/parley/define.lua:221) keeps footer stripping pure and shares it across message construction, satisfying ARCH-DRY.
./workshop/history/000166-visual-selection-definition-system-manages-footnote-close-review.md:9220:+- [lua/parley/init.lua](/Users/xianxu/workspace/parley.nvim/lua/parley/init.lua:1640) leaves Neovim IO in `render_definition` while the document transform lives in `define.lua`, satisfying ARCH-PURE.
./workshop/history/000166-visual-selection-definition-system-manages-footnote-close-review.md:9221:+- [tests/unit/build_messages_spec.lua](/Users/xianxu/workspace/parley.nvim/tests/unit/build_messages_spec.lua:707) covers user/assistant stripping and ordinary `---` preservation.
./workshop/history/000166-visual-selection-definition-system-manages-footnote-close-review.md:9222:+- [tests/unit/build_messages_spec.lua](/Users/xianxu/workspace/parley.nvim/tests/unit/build_messages_spec.lua:1605) adds live `build_messages_from_model` coverage, avoiding the #155 seam gap.
./workshop/history/000166-visual-selection-definition-system-manages-footnote-close-review.md:9225:+- [lua/parley/define.lua](/Users/xianxu/workspace/parley.nvim/lua/parley/define.lua:276): `apply_definition_footnote` always inserts `[^id]` after the selected span. If the line is already `ASIN[^asin] is here` and the user re-defines `ASIN`, it produces `ASIN[^asin][^asin] is here`. That violates the spec’s redefinition behavior in [workshop/issues/000166-visual-selection-definition-system-manages-footnote.md](/Users/xianxu/workspace/parley.nvim/workshop/issues/000166-visual-selection-definition-system-manages-footnote.md:44) and under-delivers ARCH-PURPOSE. Fix sketch: detect an existing immediate `ref` after the selected span and skip reinserting it while still updating the footer; adjust `diagnostic_span.end_col` accordingly; add pure and integration regression tests for this exact flow.
./workshop/history/000166-visual-selection-definition-system-manages-footnote-close-review.md:9228:+- [README.md](/Users/xianxu/workspace/parley.nvim/README.md:118): README still says visual `<M-CR>` inline definitions write “nothing” to the transcript. This issue changes user-facing behavior to durable `term[^id]` plus managed footer, so the README docs gate is missing an update for that surface.
./workshop/history/000166-visual-selection-definition-system-manages-footnote-close-review.md:9231:+- [workshop/plans/000166-visual-selection-definition-system-manages-footnote-plan.md](/Users/xianxu/workspace/parley.nvim/workshop/plans/000166-visual-selection-definition-system-manages-footnote-plan.md:17): Core concept tables omit an explicit `Kind` column, though the headings separate pure and integration entities.

./workshop/history/000166-visual-selection-definition-system-manages-footnote-close-review.md:9556:- **Vim ex-commands that take an implicit current-buffer arg (`:undojoin`, `:write`, `:edit`, etc.) silently target the wrong buffer when called from async/scheduled callbacks.** `helpers.undojoin(buf)` accepted a buf param but called `vim.cmd.undojoin` directly — `:undojoin` operates on the current buffer, ignoring the param. The streaming path looked like it worked because users stay focused on the chat buffer during streaming; the longer-cadence spinner timer was more likely to fire during transient focus changes (autocmds, window switches), and its joins silently went to the wrong buffer. Fix: wrap in `vim.api.nvim_buf_call(buf, function() vim.cmd.undojoin() end)`. Rule: any helper that takes a `buf` parameter and dispatches a Vim ex-command must use `nvim_buf_call` — passing the param to the helper without enforcing buffer context is a contract the helper isn't actually upholding. Spotted in #80 second-pass debugging.
./workshop/history/000166-visual-selection-definition-system-manages-footnote-close-review.md:9557:- **Sanitized snapshot in `M.get_agent` (init.lua:3570) is an allow-list, not a passthrough.** Every new field added to the agent config schema must also be appended to this snapshot, or it is silently dropped before `agent_info.resolve` ever sees it. This bit #81 (tools/max_tool_iterations/tool_result_max_bytes) and bit #118 again (synthetic_system_prompt/synthetic_system_prompt_ack) — same vector. Rule: when adding a new agent-config field, grep for `M.get_agent = function` and add it there too; ship a regression test that walks `agent record → get_agent → get_agent_info → final usage` (see `tests/unit/config_tools_spec.lua` "get_agent forwards synthetic_system_prompt config" for the pattern).
./workshop/history/000166-visual-selection-definition-system-manages-footnote-close-review.md:9564:- **In autocmd callbacks, use `nvim_buf_get_name(buf)` not `ev.file`.** `ev.file` can be a relative path when the user opened the file with a relative path (e.g. `nvim workshop/file.md`). `nvim_buf_get_name(buf)` always returns the absolute path. This caused `not_chat()` to fail silently because `find_chat_root` couldn't match the relative path against configured roots.
./workshop/history/000166-visual-selection-definition-system-manages-footnote-close-review.md:9565:- **After `nvim_buf_set_name` + rename, do `write!` then `edit!`.** `nvim_buf_set_name` marks the buffer as a "new file" at the new path. Without `edit!` to reload, the next manual `:w` warns "file already exists". The `write!` forces the initial write, and `edit!` clears the new-file flag.
./workshop/history/000166-visual-selection-definition-system-manages-footnote-close-review.md:9593:- Fast-event callbacks: no direct `nvim_*` APIs — use `vim.schedule`
./workshop/history/000166-visual-selection-definition-system-manages-footnote-close-review.md:9619:- After `edit`, use `nvim_get_current_buf()` not stale buffer variable
./workshop/history/000166-visual-selection-definition-system-manages-footnote-close-review.md:9656:- **Run the FULL `make test` (lint + unit + integration) before claiming "suite green" in `--verified` — running specs individually skips the lint gate.** This session I ran each `PlenaryBustedFile` spec directly (all green) and wrote "go test/full suite green" in the close evidence — but never ran `make test`, whose FIRST target is `lint` (luacheck). A new `while pos <= #line do` (every branch returns → luacheck 542 "loop executed at most once") failed luacheck, so `make test` was RED at the gate while my Log claimed green. The boundary review caught it (FIX-THEN-SHIP), but nothing *prevented* the premature claim. Rule: the evidence for "green" is a full `make test` exit 0, not a hand-picked set of specs; individual `PlenaryBustedFile` runs are for the red→green TDD loop, not the final gate. (Bonus: luacheck flags a `while` whose body always returns — use `if` and let the iterator's repeated closure-calls do the looping.)
./workshop/history/000166-visual-selection-definition-system-manages-footnote-close-review.md:9661:- **Raw `nvim_buf_set_text` is arch-forbidden (empty allow-list in `tests/arch/buffer_mutation_spec.lua`); `nvim_buf_set_lines` is allowed in `init.lua`.** #90's buffer-mutation boundary confines span edits to `buffer_edit.lua` (set_text allow-list is `{}` = zero uses). My first R1 cut used `nvim_buf_set_text` for the `[term]` wrap → `make test` red on the arch spec (lint was clean, so I only caught it at the full-suite run, not the unit loop). Fix: wrap via `nvim_buf_set_lines` (rewrite the affected whole line(s)) — the same primitive `drill_in_visual` already uses to wrap a selection, and it's on the set_lines allow-list for `init.lua`. Rule: before reaching for `nvim_buf_set_text`/`set_lines` in a non-`buffer_edit` file, check `tests/arch/buffer_mutation_spec.lua` — prefer the set_lines whole-line rewrite that existing wrappers use.
./workshop/history/000166-visual-selection-definition-system-manages-footnote-close-review.md:9662:- **To make a decoration-only action undoable, anchor it to a text edit + reuse `projection` — `u` reverts text, never extmarks/diagnostics.** The operator wanted the define highlight+diagnostic undoable; native `u` can't touch pure decorations (review's are undoable only because a round edits text). Resolution: a minimal `[term]` bracket (one `set_lines` = one undo entry) as the anchor + reuse `skills/review/projection.lua` (`record_empty_for(pre)` + `record(post)` + `ensure_watch`). Caveat that shaped the design: `skill_render.snapshot`/`apply_snapshot` are **line-granular**, so the highlight must be whole-line `DiffChange` to round-trip; a column-precise span would not survive undo/redo. In headless tests, `:undo` does NOT fire `TextChanged` — `nvim_exec_autocmds("TextChanged", {buffer=buf})` to drive the projection watcher deterministically.

" Minimal Neovim init for headless test runs.
" Usage: nvim --headless --noplugin -u tests/minimal_init.vim -c "PlenaryBustedDirectory tests/ {sequential=true}"

set nocompatible

" Add parley.nvim root to runtimepath so require("parley") resolves
set rtp+=.

" Add plenary (installed via lazy.nvim)
if exists('$NVIM_TEST_PLENARY') && !empty($NVIM_TEST_PLENARY)
  execute 'set rtp+=' . fnameescape($NVIM_TEST_PLENARY)
else
  set rtp+=~/.local/share/nvim/lazy/plenary.nvim
endif

" Load plenary plugin so PlenaryBusted* commands are registered
runtime plugin/plenary.vim

" Hermetic test runtime: avoid swap/temp writes outside workspace
set noswapfile
set directory=.test-tmp//
let g:parley_test_mode = v:true

`tests/minimal_init.vim` still leaves `stdpath("data")` at the real user data dir, which makes these integration specs fail in this sandbox when `file_tracker` initializes. I’m rerunning with `XDG_DATA_HOME` redirected into the workspace so the test environment matches the repo’s intended hermetic setup.

diff --git a/lua/parley/define.lua b/lua/parley/define.lua
index b50eebe..e0e42e9 100644
--- a/lua/parley/define.lua
+++ b/lua/parley/define.lua
@@ -189,6 +189,78 @@ local function managed_footer_start(lines)
     return nil
 end
 
+local function parse_footnote_line(line)
+    local id, definition = trim(line):match("^%[%^([^%]]+)%]:%s*(.-)%s*$")
+    if not id then
+        return nil
+    end
+    definition = trim(definition)
+    if definition == "" then
+        definition = "(no definition)"
+    end
+    return id, definition
+end
+
+local function is_term_byte(ch)
+    return ch:match("[%w_-]") ~= nil
+end
+
+local function expand_term_start(line, ref_start)
+    local start = ref_start
+    while start > 1 and is_term_byte(line:sub(start - 1, start - 1)) do
+        start = start - 1
+    end
+    return start
+end
+
+--- Derive persisted definition diagnostics from inline footnote references and
+--- the final managed definition footer.
+--- @param lines string[]
+--- @return table[] diagnostics with 0-based columns
+function M.footnote_diagnostics(lines)
+    lines = lines or {}
+    local footer = managed_footer_start(lines)
+    if not footer then
+        return {}
+    end
+
+    local definitions = {}
+    for i = footer + 1, #lines do
+        local id, definition = parse_footnote_line(lines[i] or "")
+        if id then
+            definitions[id] = definition
+        end
+    end
+
+    local diagnostics = {}
+    for lnum = 1, footer - 1 do
+        local line = lines[lnum] or ""
+        local search = 1
+        while true do
+            local ref_start, ref_end, id = line:find("%[%^([^%]]+)%]", search)
+            if not ref_start then
+                break
+            end
+            local definition = definitions[id]
+            if definition then
+                local term_start = expand_term_start(line, ref_start)
+                local term = line:sub(term_start, ref_start - 1)
+                table.insert(diagnostics, {
+                    id = id,
+                    term = term ~= "" and term or nil,
+                    definition = definition,
+                    lnum = lnum - 1,
+                    col = term_start - 1,
+                    end_lnum = lnum - 1,
+                    end_col = ref_end,
+                })
+            end
+            search = ref_end + 1
+        end
+    end
+    return diagnostics
+end
+
 local function split_text_lines(text)
     text = text or ""
     local lines = {}

             _parley.display_agent(buf, file_name)

             -- Disable markdown strikethrough in chat buffers — tilde (~) in file

@@ -1084,6 +1086,7 @@ M.setup_buf_handler = function()

             -- shows — a bare ~X~ or a `~/path` tilde must not cross out text.
@@ -1105,10 +1108,12 @@ M.setup_buf_handler = function()
             _parley.display_agent(buf, file_name)
             interview.highlight_timestamps(buf)
             timezone_diagnostics.refresh_buffer(buf)
+            skill_render.refresh_footnote_diagnostics(buf)
         -- Handle non-chat markdown files
         elseif _parley.is_markdown(buf, file_name) then
             interview.highlight_timestamps(buf)
             timezone_diagnostics.refresh_buffer(buf)
+            skill_render.refresh_footnote_diagnostics(buf)
         end
     end, gid)
 
@@ -1119,6 +1124,7 @@ M.setup_buf_handler = function()
         end
         if _parley._parley_bufs[buf] then
             timezone_diagnostics.refresh_buffer(buf)
+            skill_render.refresh_footnote_diagnostics(buf)
         end
     end, gid)

diff --git a/lua/parley/skill_render.lua b/lua/parley/skill_render.lua
index 12d2719..3b32639 100644
--- a/lua/parley/skill_render.lua
+++ b/lua/parley/skill_render.lua
@@ -9,6 +9,7 @@ local M = {}
 
 local DIAG_NS = "parley_skill"
 local HL_NS = "parley_skill_hl"
+local FOOTNOTE_SOURCE = "parley-footnote"
 
 local diag_ns_id
 local hl_ns_id
@@ -102,6 +103,48 @@ function M.format_diagnostic_message(text, width)
     return M.wrap(text, width or M.diagnostic_wrap_width())
 end
 
+local function is_footnote_diagnostic(diagnostic)
+    local user_data = diagnostic.user_data or {}
+    return diagnostic.source == FOOTNOTE_SOURCE or user_data.parley_kind == "footnote"
+end
+
+--- Rehydrate persisted managed markdown footnotes into Parley diagnostics.
+--- Existing non-footnote diagnostics in the shared namespace are preserved.
+--- @param buf number|nil
+function M.refresh_footnote_diagnostics(buf)
+    ensure_namespaces()
+    buf = buf or vim.api.nvim_get_current_buf()
+    if not vim.api.nvim_buf_is_valid(buf) then
+        return
+    end
+
+    local define = require("parley.define")
+    local lines = vim.api.nvim_buf_get_lines(buf, 0, -1, false)
+    local width = M.diagnostic_wrap_width()
+    local diagnostics = {}
+
+    for _, existing in ipairs(vim.diagnostic.get(buf, { namespace = diag_ns_id })) do
+        if not is_footnote_diagnostic(existing) then
+            table.insert(diagnostics, existing)
+        end
+    end
+
+    for _, footnote in ipairs(define.footnote_diagnostics(lines)) do
+        table.insert(diagnostics, {
+            lnum = footnote.lnum,
+            col = footnote.col,
+            end_lnum = footnote.end_lnum or footnote.lnum,
+            end_col = footnote.end_col,
+            message = define.format_definition(footnote.term or footnote.id, footnote.definition, width),
+            severity = vim.diagnostic.severity.INFO,
+            source = FOOTNOTE_SOURCE,
+            user_data = { parley_kind = "footnote" },
+        })
+    end
+
+    vim.diagnostic.set(diag_ns_id, buf, diagnostics)
+end
+
 --- Attach INFO diagnostics from edit explanations. Each diagnostic spans the
 --- edit's line range (lnum..end_lnum) so "cursor in the region" matches, and its
 --- message is hard-wrapped to the window's usable width for `virtual_lines`
@@ -229,6 +272,8 @@ function M.snapshot(buf)
             end_lnum = d.end_lnum,
             end_col = d.end_col,
             message = d.message,
+            source = d.source,
+            user_data = d.user_data,
         })
     end
     return { hl_lines = hl_lines, hl_spans = hl_spans, diags = diags }
@@ -257,7 +302,8 @@ function M.apply_snapshot(buf, snap)
                 end_col = d.end_col,
                 message = d.message,
                 severity = vim.diagnostic.severity.INFO,
-                source = "parley-skill",
+                source = d.source or "parley-skill",
+                user_data = d.user_data,
             })
         end
         vim.diagnostic.set(diag_ns_id, buf, diagnostics)

workshop/plans/000172-markdown-footnote-diagnostics-plan.md:5:**Goal:** Rehydrate persisted managed markdown footnotes into Parley diagnostics whenever chat or markdown buffers are entered or refreshed.
workshop/plans/000172-markdown-footnote-diagnostics-plan.md:7:**Architecture:** Add pure extraction in `define.lua` that derives diagnostic records from the existing final managed-footer shape (ARCH-PURE). Add a thin Neovim integration in `skill_render.lua` that publishes those records in Parley's diagnostic namespace using the shared diagnostic formatter (ARCH-DRY). Wire both chat and non-chat markdown lifecycle hooks so persisted footnotes display after reopen/reenter, not only immediately after define writes them (ARCH-PURPOSE).
workshop/plans/000172-markdown-footnote-diagnostics-plan.md:22:- **MarkdownFootnoteDiagnostic** — pure diagnostic data derived from inline `[^id]` references and final managed footer definitions.
workshop/plans/000172-markdown-footnote-diagnostics-plan.md:23:  - **Relationships:** N:1 with a markdown buffer; one managed footer can define many IDs, and each ID can produce many inline-reference diagnostics.
workshop/plans/000172-markdown-footnote-diagnostics-plan.md:25:  - **Future extensions:** If Parley later supports non-final or multi-line footnotes, this extractor is the one place to widen.
workshop/plans/000172-markdown-footnote-diagnostics-plan.md:27:  - **Relationships:** 1:1 with the final managed footer in a buffer.
workshop/plans/000172-markdown-footnote-diagnostics-plan.md:28:  - **DRY rationale:** Do not invent a second definition of "managed footer".
workshop/plans/000172-markdown-footnote-diagnostics-plan.md:38:- **FootnoteDiagnosticRefresh** — reads buffer lines, calls `define.footnote_diagnostics`, formats messages, and publishes diagnostics in `parley_skill`.

The diff fulfills #172: persisted managed footnotes are parsed from the existing final-footer shape, published through the shared Parley diagnostic namespace/formatter, and refreshed from chat plus non-chat markdown lifecycle hooks. I found no blocking or non-blocking findings.

1. Strengths:
- [define.lua](/Users/xianxu/workspace/parley.nvim/lua/parley/define.lua:220) keeps extraction pure and reuses the existing managed-footer detector.
- [skill_render.lua](/Users/xianxu/workspace/parley.nvim/lua/parley/skill_render.lua:114) preserves non-footnote diagnostics while replacing stale footnote diagnostics.
- [highlighter.lua](/Users/xianxu/workspace/parley.nvim/lua/parley/highlighter.lua:1060) wires refresh through BufEnter, WinEnter, TextChanged/TextChangedI, and BufWritePost.
- [atlas/chat/inline_define.md](/Users/xianxu/workspace/parley.nvim/atlas/chat/inline_define.md:7) and [atlas/index.md](/Users/xianxu/workspace/parley.nvim/atlas/index.md:15) cover the new architectural behavior.

2. Critical findings: none.

3. Important findings: none.

4. Minor findings: none.

5. Test coverage notes:
- Verified `make test` passes with XDG paths redirected into `.test-tmp`.
- Focused specs also pass: `tests/unit/define_spec.lua`, `tests/integration/highlighting_spec.lua`, `tests/integration/define_spec.lua`.
- Direct bare `nvim --headless` failed under sandbox due real `stdpath("data")` writes; rerun with test init plus XDG redirection was clean.

6. Architectural notes:
- ARCH-DRY: pass. Footer shape and diagnostic formatting are reused rather than redefined.
- ARCH-PURE: pass. Parsing/column math is pure in `define.lua`; Neovim IO stays in `skill_render.lua`/`highlighter.lua`.
- ARCH-PURPOSE: pass. The issue’s purpose is covered for immediate define render, chat markdown, non-chat markdown, and stale refresh.

7. Plan revision recommendations: none.
